key_items: Bitcoin's momentum is fading with support levels at $74,000-$76,000 at risk of triggering deeper correction
Bitcoin ETF outflows exceed $1 billion amid Treasury yield pressure and professional investor deleveraging
Trump administration pushes Fed to review crypto firms' payment rail access, creating regulatory tailwinds offset by near-term technical weakness
reasoning: Bitcoin faces significant near-term technical headwinds with momentum fading below $77,000 and key support levels weakening, compounded by $1B+ ETF outflows and crowded trade unwinding. Positive regulatory developments (South Carolina CBDC ban, Fed payment rails review, crypto compliance progress) and long-term bullish analyst targets ($255K by year-end) provide foundational support but are insufficient to counteract immediate swing-trading downside risk.
risks: Loss of $74,000-$76,000 support could trigger cascading liquidations and a move toward $70,000 as macro tests fail and leverage unwinds accelerate.
